response.justification: Bogdan has a broad software background with tangential relevance — geospatial platform work through GISCollective and distributed, high-throughput systems at Sociomantic — but his core stack is JavaScript/TypeScript and Node.js, not C++ or Rust. His C++ exposure amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project, far short of the senior-level commercial C++ proficiency this role demands. There is no evidence of routing tile pipeline work, navigation guidance systems, real-time traffic data integration, or the geospatial-at-scale data engineering that defines this position.
strengths: Geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective open-source platform and Green Map GIS application, demonstrating familiarity with geo-data concepts
Distributed, high-throughput systems experience at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency requirements
Strong Linux background and proven polyglot engineering ability across many environments and language paradigms
concerns: Minimal C++ experience — approximately six months at National Instruments plus a thesis project — well below senior commercial C++ proficiency required by the role
No Rust experience present anywhere in the resume, eliminating the acceptable alternative language path
Geospatial work is at the GIS application and data collection layer with no evidence of routing tiles, navigation pipelines, or real-time traffic signal integration

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation pipeline experience
